The following table presents a comprehensive analysis of the performance metrics associated with various roles within the
county governance framework, specifically focusing on the position of county commissioner. As a pivotal role in the local
political landscape, the county commissioner is responsible for overseeing the county council's operations and ensuring
accountability to the assembly. The data collected from respondents provides critical insights into the latency and mean
amplitude measurements, which are essential for evaluating the effectiveness and responsiveness of commissioners in their
decision-making processes. Notably, the "Latency (ms) (range)" metric reflects the time taken by commissioners to respond to
key issues, with an average of 28.70 ± 1.66 ms for the standard range. Additionally, the "Mean amplitude (μV)" values, such as
3.60 ± 1.07 μV, shed light on the cognitive engagement levels of these officials during deliberations. Together, these metrics
offer a nuanced understanding of the operational dynamics within county governance, highlighting areas for potential
improvement and strategic development.
